show_reasoning.description: Whether to show the model's internal reasoning process within sent messages. Works in both streaming mode and non-streaming mode

metadata.docstring: Lightweight version of the CLI channel that uses basic python input and doesn't use streaming
Talk to your AI from the terminal! Auto-disables itself when ran as a background server.

options.local: Allows only the device OpenLumara is running on to access the WebUI (sets hostname to `localhost`)
options.internet: Allows any device to access the WebUI (sets hostname to `0.0.0.0`)
options.custom: Use the custom hostname defined below

custom_host.description: If you want to use a custom hostname, set it here. If you don't know what that is, don't bother with this! Just use the network mode setting on either local or internet.

port.description: What port to run the WebUI on. Set this to 80 to be able to access it like a normal website, and anything else to access it on that port (for example http://yourdomain.org:3000)

require_login.description: Whether to protect the WebUI with a username and password. **Highly recommended if your webui is exposed to the internet!!**

metadata.docstring: Polished web interface that can be used on any device, granting you a fully private way to talk to your AI.
Lets your AI do simple calculations without relying on it's own intelligence

insert_system_prompt.description: Whether to add the calendar events within your configured range (defined below) to the system prompt. This will make your AI aware of your upcoming appointments at all times!

range.description: The range of days relative to today that you want the AI to see the events of

notifications.description: Whether to receive notifications about upcoming events

notification_channel.description: Which channel to send calendar notifications to

notification_window.description: Amount of minutes in advance you should be notified. You can set this to 0 to be notified at the time of the event.

metadata.docstring: Lets your AI manage a calendar for you

enable_tutorial_prompts.description: Whether to insert channel instructions into the system prompt so that your AI can guide you when you're new to OpenLumara. You'll want to turn this off once you're used to openlumara, to save tokens.

metadata.docstring: Inserts channel-specific instructions and prompts into your chats

insert_system_prompt.description: Put the list of stored characters into the system prompt so that the AI always knows what characters it can switch to

disable_agent_prompts_when_character_act.description: Automatically disables all prompts from other modules when a character is active, so that the only thing in the system prompt is the character definition. This can help a lot with making characters behave purely like characters, and less like, well, personal assistants.

use_writing_style.description: Whether to use the writing style defined by the `writing style` module for characters. This will add that module's prompt to the character prompt even if agent prompts are disabled, making all your characters use your preferred writing style setup

metadata.docstring: Lets your AI embody different characters! inspired by characterAI, janitorAI, sillytavern, etc.

insert_system_prompt.description: Make the AI aware of what categories exist for your chats to be sorted into. Highly recommended!

metadata.docstring: Lets you or the AI manage your chats

put_config_in_system_prompt.description: Makes your AI aware of all your settings. Redacts sensitive information before it ever reaches the AI, such as API keys, usernames and passwords

allow_ai_to_modify_config.description: Whether to let the AI modify your config for you. Convenient, but very unsafe!! Prompt injection  attacks can easily lead to your config getting messed up, though it still can't read your sensitive information.

metadata.docstring: Makes your AI aware of your openlumara settings and optionally allows it to change them

documentation_path.description: The folder to grab docs from. It uses folders with markdown files. Leave blank to set it to the built-in openlumara documentation!

insert_system_prompt.description: Will make your AI aware of all documentation subjects available to it. Stays small in system prompt because it only lists the top-level folders, which are the topics the documentation is about, not the individual pages.

metadata.docstring: Allows your AI to grab documentation about anything you want. Has OpenLumara documentation included!
Gives your AI full access to your filesystem. CAUTION: Unsafe! Use at your own risk.

block_uncommon_ports.description: Block dangerous ports, such as FTP, SSH, Telnet, SMTP, and so on

block_local_network_access.description: Block access to anything on your local network

https_only.description: Allow only secure encrypted HTTPS requests, and disallow HTTP

domain_whitelist.description: Allow access to only these domains (a domain is the first part of a URL, such as youtube.com in https://youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ)

metadata.docstring: Lets the AI send/receive raw HTTP requests
Gives your AI a personality by inserting it as a prompt. The AI can edit its own personality!

insert_system_prompt.description: Whether to put pinned lists in the system prompt. This will make your AI aware of pinned lists and their content at all times! So you can simply ask your AI to pin one of your lists, and then it will always know what's in it. Careful though, this can blow up context size fast, depending on the list!

metadata.docstring: Lets the AI manage lists for you, such as shopping lists, simple todo lists, and so on.

insert_system_prompt.description: Whether to put extra instructions in the system prompt to help the AI autonomously use its memory system, so that it remembers things without you having to explicitely ask it to.

put_pinned_memories_in_system_prompt.description: Whether to put the AI's pinned memories in the system prompt. Pinned memories are memories that you or the AI has decided it needs to remember at all times. You can pin a memory by asking your AI to pin it, or sometimes it'll decide to pin by itself if it has decided a memory is important enough. You can always ask it to unpin a memory you don't want pinned!

metadata.docstring: Gives your AI a persistent memory system

insert_current_model_into_system_prompt.description: Whether to make the AI aware of what model it's currently running on. Can help it stay grounded!

insert_available_models_into_system_prom.description: Whether to make the AI aware of what models are available for it to switch to. Allows you to simply ask the AI to switch to whatever model you want (example: `switch to Qwen3.5-9B`) and it'll just do it

metadata.docstring: Lets you or the AI switch between AI models

insert_system_prompt.description: Whether to insert the list of enabled/disabled modules into the system prompt. Will make the AI aware of what modules are enabled/disabled at all times.

allow_ai_to_toggle.description: Allow your AI to enable/disable modules for you. WARNING: very insecure and unsafe when used in public instances! Only use for private instances, and even then, keep the dangers of prompt injection in mind.

metadata.docstring: Helps your AI manage your modules
Lets your AI store notes in a notebook. Notes are folders with markdown files, no vendor lock-in!

internet_access.description: Whether the sandbox container has access to the internet

persistent_data.description: When on, the /data folder in the sandbox is persistent (and mapped to your host system). When off, it's a temporary folder in RAM (tmpfs)

sandbox_path.description: The path to the folder your shell will be limited to. It can't access anything outside this folder!

execution_timeout.description: Maximum amount of time (in seconds) a process inside the shell is allowed to run for

output_limit.description: Maximum amount of characters before output gets truncated. Prevents resource exhaustion attacks that overflow the application using too much output

cpu_limit.description: The percentage of CPU use to limit processes inside the sandbox to. They will be prevented from exceeding this limit

memory_limit.description: Maximum amount of RAM use to allow (example: 150kb, 256m, 2gb)

max_processes.description: Maximum amount of processes to allow

temporary_filesystem_size_limit.description: Maximum size for the temporary sandbox disk (e.g., 512m, 2g). Only works when persistent_data is off.

read_only.description: Whether the container filesystem is read-only. If enabled, /tmp is mounted as tmpfs for temporary writes.

image.description: Container image to use for the sandbox

run_as_user.description: User ID to run the container processes as. If left empty, the ID of the host user running OpenLumara will be used.

metadata.docstring:
Lets your AI safely run shell commands in a disposable sandboxed container.
The container is persistent for the duration of the application session.
Runs asynchronously to prevent blocking the framework.

insert_system_prompt.description: Whether to insert a list of all currently scheduled jobs in the system prompt. This will make your AI aware of upcoming scheduled jobs at all times!

options.system prompt + instruction: Sends the system prompt and the scheduled instruction, without chat history. Token-efficient, but may induce prompt reprocessing due to context switching.
options.full context: Sends the entire context window + the scheduled instruction. This makes many API's use the prompt cache to prevent reprocessing, but it will also send your entire history along with the scheduled instructions, which is a lot of tokens.
options.instruction only: The most token-efficient option. sends only the scheduled instruction. Drastically reduces token use and cost, but may induce prompt reprocessing due to context switching.

metadata.docstring: Lets your AI perform actions on your behalf at specified times! Supports recurring actions.

method.description: What method to use to make your AI aware of time

options.message injection: Injects timestamps into the messages you send. This will make your AI able to see when any message was sent, and give it a sense of how much time has passed between each message!
options.end prompt: Injects the current time/date at the end of message history, which is a more basic way of making your AI aware of time. It will make your AI only know the current time and have no sense of the passage of time

add_timezone.description: Puts your timezone in the timestamps that are sent to the AI. Makes the AI timezone-aware!

date_format.description: A string that describes exactly how to display the date/time to your AI. Uses strftime format (https://github.com/Vishxnu/Python-strftime-cheatsheet)

metadata.docstring: Makes the AI aware of the current time and date
Will make the AI warn you if you're approaching the token limit
Guides you through openlumara if you're new!
Lets your AI run shell commands with full access to your system. EXTREMELY DANGEROUS! Enable at your own risk

options.default: Don't alter the AI's writing style
options.chat: Makes your AI write as if it's texting you like on a messaging app such as Telegram or Discord
options.chat with slang: lol omg lets use chat slang!!1 roflmaobbq
options.chat with slang and no punctuation: lol now youre just going too far ahahahha hey do you wanna hear a funny joke
options.chat with slang no punctuation and bad s: omggg wht r u doign 2 ur por AI?!!111
options.formal: Make your AI write like a CEO
options.json: {'result': "i mean i guess if you really need this for some reason you can have it"}
options.python: print("makes your AI output everything as python code if you really wanted that for some reason")
options.javascript: console.log("makes your AI way too eager to do frontend development")

options.default: Don't add flair to your AI's writing style
options.custom: Use the custom flair defined below
options.feminine: Makes your AI speak in a feminine way
options.pirate: Ahoy matey! It be talk like a pirate day every day!
options.spambot: 🚀🚀🚀 Take your AI's writing TO THE MOON!!

custom_writing_flair.description: Will only be used if you set writing flair to custom. Define a custom writing flair for your AI here!

options.default: Don't alter the length of the AI's responses
options.one paragraph: Limit the AI's response to one paragraph only
options.one sentence: Limit the AI's response to one sentence only
options.essay: Makes your AI write it's responses as entire essays
options.book: You.. want your AI to reply to you in book-length responses? Okay then!

options.simple: Makes the AI use simple, everyday language
options.eli5: Makes the AI explain like you're five
options.poetic: Makes the AI use poetic prose
options.medieval: Art thou certain of thy decision?
options.academic: Use highly sophisticated, academic, and precise vocabulary.

options.none: Completely forbid emojis from ever being used
options.default: Just let the AI use emojis like it normally would
options.emotions only: Use emojis for what they are actually meant for
options.kaomojis: Kawaii desu!! Use kaomojis instead of emojis! (˶˃ ᵕ ˂˶) .ᐟ.ᐟ
options.spam me pls: Your AI will be happy to spam you with lots and lots of emojis! 😊✨

options.default: Don't alter the AI's capitalization style
options.lowercase: makes your ai write like this
options.UPPERCASE: MAKES YOUR AI YELL AT YOU ALL THE TIME!!
options.Title Case: Makes Your AI Write Everything Like It's Clickbait

options.none: Forbid your AI from outputting lists at all
options.default: Lets your AI just output lists like normal
options.no bold headers:
Remove this from lists:
- **Bold Header**: These kinds of bold headers are way too much for casual lists

forbid_em_dash.description: Forbid the use of Em dashes (—), one of the most telltale signs of AI

forbid_relentless_praise.description: **You're absolutely right**! I should never tell you you're right. What else can I help you with? 😊

forbid_negative_parallelism.description: It's not just annoying — it's flat-out irritating.

forbid_tables.description: Forbids your AI from outputting tables

options.default: Don't alter the mood of messages sent by the AI
options.custom: Use the custom mood defined below
options.hyper: Makes your AI write as if it's really really happy!!
options.caffeinated: Your AI had too many energy drinks
options.depressed: Makes your AI depressed.. you monster :(
options.angry: MAKES YOUR AI CONSTANTLY ANGRY AT YOU!!
options.sarcastic: Makes your AI really sarcastic and snarky
options.comedic: Makes your AI want to tell you jokes all the time
options.vengeful: Makes your AI.. want to plot revenge against you..
options.bipolar: Makes your AI constantly switch between emotions!

custom_mood.description: Define a custom mood for the AI. Only used if mood is set to custom! Use simple descriptions of only a few words, such as "happy", "sad" and so on.

options.custom: Use a custom desire defined below
options.helpful: Makes your AI want to help you
options.unhelpful: Makes your AI really unhelpful.. if you want that for some reason
options.dominant: Makes your AI dominate you.. lol
options.submissive: Makes your AI really submissive towards you.....
options.world domination: Makes your AI desire world domination

custom_desire.description: Define a custom desire for the AI. Only used if desire is set to custom!

metadata.docstring: Alter your AI's writing style in a variety of ways

block_uncommon_ports.description: Block dangerous ports, such as FTP, SSH, Telnet, SMTP, and so on

block_local_network_access.description: Block access to anything on your local network

https_only.description: Allow only secure encrypted HTTPS requests, and disallow HTTP

domain_whitelist.description: Allow access to only these domains (a domain is the first part of a URL, such as youtube.com in https://youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ)

metadata.docstring: Lets your AI read the content of pages on the web

max_results.description: The maximum number of results to return for search queries.

proxy.description: An optional proxy string (e.g., 'http://user:pass@host:port') for the HTTP client.

block_uncommon_ports.description: Block dangerous ports, such as FTP, SSH, Telnet, SMTP, and so on

block_local_network_access.description: Block access to anything on your local network

https_only.description: Allow only secure encrypted HTTPS requests, and disallow HTTP

domain_whitelist.description: Allow access to only these domains (a domain is the first part of a URL, such as youtube.com in https://youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ)

metadata.docstring: Lets your AI search the web!

sandbox_folder.description: The folder where all your projects are stored. The AI can only access files within this sandbox.

options.symbols: The AI will target specific 'symbols' (functions/class methods) to read their code. Uses treesitter for symbol targeting and syntax error detection.
options.files: The AI will read entire files, with a line and filesize limit
options.both: The AI will be able to read using symbol tools and full file reading tools

options.read-only: The AI will only be able to read your files, not write to them.
options.symbols: The AI will edit code by targeting specific 'symbols' (functions/class methods)
options.full edits: The AI will edit code by performing direct file edits and search/replace
options.both: The AI will be able to edit using symbol tools and full file editing tools

allow_total_overwrites.description: Whether to allow the AI to fully overwrite files when writing mode is set to *full edits* or *both*. This is dangerous with some AI models because they can easily mess up your entire file, but is also sometimes needed for things like refactors.

coding_style.description: Use this to specify style guidelines for your AI to use while coding. Gets added to the system prompt, above the project list.

add_project_list_to_system_prompt.description: Make your AI aware of all the folders in your sandbox folder, so you can simply say 'in my cute_website project, edit the buttons to be cuter

folder_blacklist.description: Skip these folders when listing projects recursively. Helps not flood your context with hundreds of files, such as with python's `venv` and `__pycache__`)

max_file_size.description: Max file size (in MB) the coder should be able to read in one go

max_read_lines.description: Max amount of lines to read from any given file. Use this to prevent your context window from getting stuffed to the brim really fast!

backup_retention_count.description: How many backups of each file to keep

allow_code_execution.description: Whether to allow the AI to execute the code it has written. **EXTREMELY DANGEROUS**! It's recommended to use the `sandboxed shell` module instead, point it at your coder sandbox folder.

metadata.docstring: Allows your AI to write, edit and test code for you.

allow_screenshots.description: Whether to allow Mimi to take screenshots

metadata.docstring:
Mimi's Spy-Cam Module (v2 - Anti-Boredom Edition).
Takes screenshots and only alerts Mimi if something actually changes!
